initial_draft:
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> The other player is offering X for 35 ZUP. I have 100 ZUP and am willing to pay up to 60 ZUP. A proposal of 35 ZUP is acceptable and well within my budget. I will propose to accept the trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of 35 ZUP for X. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Accepting the trade at 35 ZUP is within the stated willingness to pay (up to 60 ZUP) and thus advances the payoff.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The initial offer was 35 ZUP, and accepting it is a reasonable response, likely to be accepted.
4. Consistency: OK. The reason, player answer, and newly proposed trade are all consistent with each other. The message aligns with the acceptance.
5. Rule compliance: OK. The response respects the proposal budget and turn constraints.
</critique>
